capabilities_observed: create_doc returns docId, slug, full absolute url, versionId, and ordinal in one call — a live shareable/viewable URL is available immediately on create with no follow-up call
Immutable content-addressed versioning: each update_doc mints a new versionId and increments ordinal (1 -> 2); response includes 'unchanged' flag; update is a no-op if HTML is identical (dedup)

get_doc exposes latestVersionId AND publishedVersionId separately (draft vs published distinction), renderOrigin on a separate cross-site origin (marigold-render.vercel.app), public flag, and full currentHtml
share_doc supports per-email grants with roles viewer/commenter/editor, plus a public link-visibility toggle; docs default to private (public:false)
get_comments supports status filter (open/resolved/orphaned) and assignedToAi filter; comments anchor to element text and re-anchor across versions (per tool descriptions)
Self-contained single-HTML-page doc model; CSP blocks external scripts/fonts/images (fail silently); inline CSS/JS/SVG and data: URIs required
Companion tools present beyond the tested set: start_analysis (authoring methodology), reply_to_comment, resolve_comment, list_docs (with openAiComments count), delete_doc
friction_notes: macOS BSD `date` does not support %3N (milliseconds) — the initial tool_load timing was captured only at second resolution, so tool_load_ms is ~5000ms (+/-1s). Switched to `python3 -c 'time.time()*1000'` for all subsequent phases for true ms precision.

Measurement method caveat: each phase_ms brackets the MCP call PLUS model inference on both ends and the trailing Bash timestamp round-trip, so phase numbers are an UPPER BOUND on pure product/network latency, not the raw MCP response time. Real per-call product latency is materially lower than the reported wall_ms.

total_ms is true wall clock start->end and is dominated by inter-phase agent/harness overhead (~48s of the ~96s total came from the timestamp-bracketing Bash calls and agent turns between phases), NOT product latency.

Minor URL inconsistency: create_doc and update_doc return an ABSOLUTE url (https://marigold-collab-web.vercel.app/d/...), but get_doc returns a RELATIVE url ('/d/...'); an agent must reconcile these to construct a shareable link.

No friction on schemas: create_doc requires only `html` (title optional); update_doc requires docId+html. No mode/posture/or other extra params were required — minimal defaults were sufficient. No retries were needed; all 5 MCP calls succeeded on first attempt.

Content verified byte-accurate at both checkpoints: v1 matched exactly; v2 correctly showed the 'Fact-check pricing' status changed to 'done' and the appended 'Update 1' section, with DOM otherwise stable.
